revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:布勢神社 (さぬき市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Fuse Shrine |location = 3642 Nishiiida, Samukawa-cho, Sanuki City, Kagawa Prefecture 769-2322 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{No image|cat=Sanuki City}} |deity = {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |Foundation = Unknown (pre-Tenpyō era) |festival = 9th day of the 9th lunar month |Rank = Minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}; one of the 24 shrines in Sanuki Province listed in the Engishiki |LatDeg = 34 |LatMin = 15 |LatSec = 29.02 |LonDeg = 134 |LonMin = 12 |LonSec = 12.08 }}
'''Fuse Shrine''' (布勢神社, {{ill|Fuse Shrine|ja|布勢神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuse Shrine|qq=}}) is located at 3642 Nishiiida, Samukawa-cho, {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|qid=Q857202|lt=Sanuki|1=Sanuki, Kagawa}}, Kagawa Prefecture. It is one of the 24 shrines of Sanuki Province listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}. [[ja:布勢神社 (さぬき市)]] == Overview == Fuse Shrine is a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Ishida Shrine|ja|石田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishida Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504194|1=Ishida Shrine}}, a parish shrine of the Ishida Village. The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Sanuki Province, Samukawa District, Minor Shrine, Fuse Shrine." It is one of Sanuki Province's 24 shrines listed in the Engishiki.
The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the eldest prince of Emperor Kōgen (the 8th emperor of Japan). The Engishiki notes the existence of three Fuse Shrines across Japan—in {{ill|Imizu District, Toyama|en|Imizu District, Toyama|qid=Q6004195|lt=Imizu District|1=Imizu District, Toyama}} of Etchū Province, {{ill|Akasa District|ja|赤坂郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasa District|1=Akasaka district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11635439}} of Bizen Province, and Samukawa District of Sanuki Province—dedicated to Ōhiko-no-Mikoto<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Kagawa Prefecture Shrine Records, Vol. 1 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1227836/1/105 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-29 |publisher=National Diet Library |language=ja |author=Kagawa Prefectural Priests' Association |editor=Kagawa Prefectural Priests' Association |pages=134-135}}</ref>.
The foundation date is uncertain, but records from {{ill|Gokuraku-ji (Sanuki)|ja|極楽寺 (さぬき市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokuraku-ji (Sanuki)|1=Gokuraku-ji Temple (Sanuki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q56347327}} indicate that during the Tenpyō era, the monk {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|qid=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}} established a Yakushi Hall (now Gokuraku-ji) in Ishida and designated Fuse Shrine as its guardian deity. This suggests that the shrine predates the Tenpyō period<ref name=":0" />.
During the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era (1573–1591), Fuse Shrine was protected during the Chōsokabe clan’s military conflicts. Ishida Sabei Norihiro safeguarded the shrine, sparing it from destruction, as recorded in historical texts such as the {{ill|Sandai Monogatari|ja|三代物語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sandai Monogatari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ikoma-ki|ja|生駒記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Zensanshi|ja|全讃史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zensanshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name=":0" />.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}
== References == * Kagawa Prefectural Priests' Association, *Kagawa Prefecture Shrine Records, Vol. 1*, Kagawa Prefectural Priests' Association Publishing, 1938. ISBN JPNO46047286.
{{citation needed|ja|布勢神社|version=100150238|comment=Imported full ja history}}
